name: Input
description: Inputs capture text-based values for search, filtering, profile details, financial settings, and dashboard configuration while maintaining a quiet, premium visual voice.
summary: Use the input component for short to medium text entry. The field should feel calm and highly legible, with low visual noise at rest and strong clarity on focus or validation.

variants.name: default
variants.useCase: General profile forms, filters, and search bars.

variants.name: quiet
variants.useCase: Embedded controls inside cards or toolbars where the border should recede slightly.

variants.name: validated
variants.useCase: Forms that surface helper or error text after a submission attempt.

contentGuidelines: Labels should name the data directly: Search reports, Billing email, Renewal note.
Helper text should explain format or consequence, not repeat the label.
Error text should be specific enough to resolve in one pass.
behaviorNotes: A leading icon is appropriate for search and account lookup, but it should not replace the visible label.
Trailing actions such as Clear or Reveal password should remain textually explicit when space allows.
Read-only inputs should still look intentional and legible rather than disabled.

accessibility.labeling: Every input requires a visible label or a programmatically associated accessible name.
accessibility.validation: Error text should be connected to the field through aria-describedby.
accessibility.focus: Never remove the focus ring; the ring token is the primary keyboard affordance.

compositionNotes: Inputs work best inside cards, modal forms, and sidebar filter sections where their subtle fill can separate editable content from static text.
Because the palette is warm and low-contrast, spacing and consistent labels are essential for form clarity.
Use quiet variants when the page already contains strong KPI or chart emphasis nearby.
